reason: Global TianChen-RV MLIR stack, RVV-first authority chain, and Stage1/Stage2 boundaries
RVV selected-body route-entry family ownership, segment2 provider-plan boundary, provider route construction, fail-closed diagnostics
TCRVEmitCLowerableRoute ownership, common EmitC neutrality, metadata mirror restrictions
Plugin-owned route provider and registry/interface authority boundaries
Focused C++/lit/generated-bundle/ssh-rvv evidence requirements and metadata-only test restrictions
Plugin-local route-provider and selected-body realization review checklist
Compute ownership and RVV route shape checks
Capability/config facts constrain provider legality without becoming route authority
Task-local implementation scope, acceptance criteria, and validation plan

summary: RVVEmitCRouteProvider now requests the selected-body segment2 route-family provider plan before constructing TCRVEmitCLowerableRoute, uses the plan route id, required headers, VL/vector/mask type mappings, and ABI order, and keeps non-segment2 routes on the prior path.

Segment2 provider-plan payload now carries route-construction facts: emitCRouteID, requiredHeaders, VL/vector/mask type names, provider_supported_mirror, runtime ABI mirror, and operand-binding mirrors.

Computed-mask segment2 update, computed-mask segment2 load/store, and plain segment2 deinterleave/interleave tests now assert provider-built routes consume selected-body family/provider-plan facts, including fail-closed stale mirror diagnostics.
